content.full_text:
GABBAY ARCHITECTS
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATlON
YASSI E. GABBAY, D.P.L,G.-A.I .,
HAMID E. GABBAY. A,I,A,
June 15, 1992
Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practice Council
428 "J" Streel, Suite #5C0
Sacramento, Ca. 95814
Re: Airplane ticket paid by a client.
Dear Mr. Hallabrin,
I am a Commissioner of the Planning Commission for the City of Beverly Hills. I
am also a registered architect and a partner in the firm of Gabbay Architects.
Our firm, beside California, has jobs in places such as Nevada, West Virginia
and New York. Very otten I have to visit the job sites and usually the airplane
ticket is bought and paid for by the client.
Based on Form 721 , on which Commissioners have to report all gifts and air
tickets, I asked the F.P.P.C. and in particular Ms. Suzanne Barbarol iI these
tickels are considered gifts or should be considered income?
Ms. Barbarol, alter consulting with legal divisions, informed that these tickets
are considered income and NOT GIFTS. However, it was suggested that I get a
written opinion. I am, therefore, cordially writing to you so that you may confirm
the answer given to me.
Wilh my best regards,
)?
TEL. 6e*rwq€{9pqF . FAX 6Qrea*gt!(xxk
(:ro;s53-ss66 (310)zot_oszo
444 SOUTH CANON DRIVE SUITE 2OO. BEVERLY HILLS CA, 9O2]2

sections.question_synthetic: Are airplane tickets paid for by clients for job site visits considered gifts or income that must be reported on Form 721 by a Planning Commission member?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter from member seeking written confirmation of prior verbal guidance. No formal Q/A sections present. Document references prior FPPC phone consultation with Ms. Suzanne Barbaroli indicating tickets are income, not gifts. Requesting written opinion to confirm.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Are airplane tickets paid for by clients for job site visits considered gifts or income that must be reported on Form 721 by a Planning Commission member?

embedding.summary: Yassi E. Gabbay, a Beverly Hills Planning Commission member and registered architect, seeks written confirmation from the FPPC regarding whether airplane tickets paid by clients for job site visits should be reported as income or gifts on Form 721.
